category_title: Six-year-old students with EBD, ADHD, and ODD who exhibit noncompliance, aggression, and disrobing during difficult academic tasks
student_description: Axel is a six-year-old student who exhibits extreme behaviors, including disrobing, swearing, and throwing materials, when faced with non-preferred or difficult academic tasks. He struggles with multi-step directions and verbal reprimands but responds highly effectively to visual consequence maps and structured calm-down schedules that allow him to self-monitor and choose his behavioral path.

criteria.rationale: Axel struggles to process verbal demands when frustrated and needs a visual display of steps to self-monitor and calm down.
criteria.check: The tutor introduces, points to, or references a visual consequence map or calm-down schedule when Axel shows initial signs of resistance or escalation.

criteria.rationale: Continuing to engage verbally with Axel during extreme behaviors (swearing, disrobing) reinforces the behavior, whereas waiting allows him to process the visual maps.
criteria.check: If Axel escalates to extreme behaviors, the tutor explicitly removes attention, pauses the interaction, or waits briefly before re-engaging with the visual tools.

criteria.rationale: Axel needs explicit recognition of his successful self-regulation to reinforce the calm-down process before he can successfully re-engage with a difficult task.
criteria.check: After Axel calms down, the tutor explicitly praises his successful use of the calm-down steps before prompting him to return to the original academic task.
